page: Amazon Web Services c7g.2xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the custom AWS Graviton3 processor. Built on the arm64 architecture, it features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The processor utilizes the ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 model, equipped with 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is provisioned with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most compute and memory operations. The instance achieved a stress-ng single-core score of 1532.98 and a multi-core score of 12238.41. In memory bandwidth testing, it registered 774.89 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 146.42 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Cryptographic performance shows some variation; while AES encryption is supported, the PassMark CPU encryption test returned a weak score of 1114.02. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression was also weak at 7.55 GB/sec, though multi-threaded decompression reached an average tier of 35.95 GB/sec. For database workloads, the instance processed 13.27 million Redis SET operations per second and scored 8036.07 in PassMark database operations.

This instance is designed for compute-heavy workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores and a 1:2 CPU-to-RAM ratio. It is appropriate for tasks such as software compilation, where it scored 11625.0 in Geekbench clang compilation, static web serving with a throughput of 15.45 GB/sec, and light machine learning tasks, processing 135M parameter LLM text generation at 94.08 tokens per second. However, performance drops significantly for larger models, as seen in the poor prompt processing speed of 14.13 tokens per second for a 7B model. The absence of local storage and GPUs makes it less appropriate for heavy storage-bound databases or accelerated graphics rendering. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the custom ARM architecture offers a specialized compute-to-memory balance without the overhead of hyperthreading.
description: Amazon Web Services c7g.2xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton3 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 16.0 GB of memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show average performance across general compute, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 12238.41 and 13.27 million Redis SET operations per second. However, it exhibits weaker performance in cryptographic encryption and single-threaded decompression. This instance is appropriate for dedicated compute tasks, software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference, offering a balanced, qualitative cost-to-performance profile for ARM-native workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.2xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 16 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering balanced performance for ARM-native workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.2xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 8 Graviton3 cores, 16 GB RAM, and Nitro hypervisor virtualization.
tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and balanced memory bandwidth for efficient, non-hyperthreaded workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated physical ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 cores at 2.6 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core resource density
Nitro hypervisor with 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and no local storage
Average performance in software compilation and static web serving
Weak performance in cryptographic encryption and single-threaded decompression
Appropriate for web serving, software compilation, and light LLM inference
